items.title: Some country towns could lose more than half of their shade due to aging trees
items.summary: Some country towns could lose up to 80 per cent of their shade due to aging trees, but replacing them is not simple for councils.

items.title: Investigation launched into Papua New Guinea Defence Force recruitment processes following soldier protest
items.summary: A small group of officers deserted their posts this week in protest over alleged government corruption.

items.title: NSW nurses, midwives awarded major pay rise after decision from Industrial Relations Commission
items.summary: Registered nurses will receive a "one-off reset" of a 10 per cent pay rise in the first year, backdated to July 1, 2025, and a 3 per cent pay rise the following two years.

items.title: Fuel-saving campaign prompts open letter to prime minister from regional Queensland council
items.summary: A Queensland council sends an open letter to the Albanese government calling for a national fuel tracker to protect rural economies, while criticising the federal fuel-saving campaign.

items.title: Mother fears children may have been filmed with hidden cameras in Horsham Town Hall change rooms
items.summary: A mother says she is disturbed after learning of hidden cameras found in the theatre change rooms her children used in November with a lack of dates adding to parents' concerns.

items.title: US jury finds Ticketmaster and Live Nation have anticompetitive monopoly
items.summary: A New York jury finds concert giant Live Nation and its Ticketmaster subsidiary have a harmful monopoly over big concert venues.

items.title: Video shows father and son illegally scuttling boat off NSW South Coast
items.summary: The two fishermen were fined $15,000 for towing their vessel out to sea and illegally scuttling it.

items.title: Why Australia turned to Brunei to shore up supplies of critical crop fertiliser during Middle East war
items.summary: The Middle East conflict has disrupted the flow of Australia's imports of the fertiliser urea. Facing the potential threat of reduced crop yields, Anthony Albanese looked to Australia's neighbours.

items.title: WA wine industry warns of impact of Containers for Change expansion
items.summary: A newly formed West Australian wine group warns that bottles of wine produced in the state could increase by $1.25 if the expansion of the Containers for Change scheme goes ahead.

items.title: From a new Australian musical to buzzy horror, here's what's in cinemas in April
items.summary: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

items.title: Household spending up in March as fuel costs surge, over-65s lead spending growth, CBA data shows
items.summary: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

items.title: Refinery hit by blaze says fuel production secure, gas leak under investigation — as it happened
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says the facility is still producing diesel and jet fuel but "at reduced levels, as a precaution". Follow our coverage as it happened live.

items.title: Refinery blaze may impact Australia's petrol production 'for some time'
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says the fire in Geelong appears to be an accident at this stage and there are no suspicious circumstances.

items.title: Josh Hazlewood helps Royal Challengers Bengaluru to IPL win over Lucknow
items.summary: Josh Hazlewood has produced another masterful display in the IPL to rocket Royal Challengers Bengaluru back to the top of the league.

items.title: Global tour operator Intrepid invests millions in Central Australia's iconic Ooraminna Homestead
items.summary: Australian-based global tour operator Intrepid has announced a multi-million-dollar investment in Central Australia's Ooraminna Homestead, 30 kilometres south of Alice Springs.

items.title: WA Police stands by moderation strategy despite racist comments on social media posts
items.summary: Racist comments are being published on WA Police social media posts with little moderation, but the force says it is not prepared to switch off comments.

items.title: Analysis of 1,300 new dinosaur bones overhauls understanding of Qld fossil emblem
items.summary: The dinosaur's face lift has become the butt of online jokes among palaeontology fans, but researchers say it is the most accurate depiction of the creature yet.

items.title: Skull render of Muttaburrasaurus
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Arsenal draws with Sporting, Bayern Munich defeats Real Madrid in UEFA Champions League
items.summary: Arsenal advance to the UEFA Champions League semifinals with a goalless draw, as Bayern Munich defeat Real Madrid.

items.title: Hope Downs ruling 'clear win' for Gina Rinehart, prominent industry analyst says
items.summary: Gina Rinehart and her company, Hancock Prospecting, are the "clear winners" of what prominent Perth industry commentator Tim Treadgold said was the "big daddy" of legal battles.

items.title: Alex de Minaur loses to Hamad Medjedovic at Barcelona Open
items.summary: Alex de Minaur's uneven season has hit another road bump as Australia's number one succumbed to a shock last-16 loss at the Barcelona Open.

items.title: Woman claims 'toxic dose' of wrong drug during routine surgery at PRH left her brain damaged
items.summary: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" when she was given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

items.title: Matildas defeat Kenya 2-0 in FIFA Series, Sam Kerr scores 75th international goal
items.summary: Sam Kerr's 75th international goal has helped the Matildas to a surprisingly tough, controversial 2-0 victory over Kenya in the FIFA Series final.

items.title: With diagnosis rates steadily increasing, men need to talk about prostate cancer
items.summary: Michael Platts panicked when he was diagnosed with prostate cancer. Now he wants to help others break through the stigma and silence surrounding the condition.

items.title: Albury Wodonga Health review identifies series of errors before man's death by suicide
items.summary: Blake Whittington, 23, was taken to hospital after a suicide attempt. He was discharged, then referred to a support service that did not contact him until days after he took his own life.

items.title: Coroner considers if partner contributed to man's death after life support was switched off
items.summary: The Coroner's Court of Victoria is investigating whether the partner of a dying Royal Melbourne Hospital patient contributed to his death in 2021.

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication
items.summary: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

items.title: Victorian farmer left feeling 'disrespected' by VicGrid officers for denying access to land
items.summary: VicGrid escalates its attempts to access land for VNI West surveys, threatening fines or court-ordered entry to farmers who stand in the way.

items.title: South-west Qld councils pitch community insurance mutual after premiums rise by 500 per cent
items.summary: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

items.title: Invasive long-spined sea urchin found on Tasmania's west coast sparks fears for marine habitat
items.summary: The discovery on Tasmania's west coast has scientists and fishers alike fearing the spread of a destructive species to another previously untouched part of the state's marine environment.

items.title: Albanese government's frustration rises alongside fuel prices and inflation
items.summary: Fuel prices and inflation aren't the only metrics climbing as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut. The Albanese government's level of frustration is rising just as fast.

items.title: These fuel supply dashboards claim to know what's really going on. Can you trust them?
items.summary: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation raises questions about popular independent fuel security dashboards that have emerged online, including one whose creators are linked to the Liberal Party.

items.title: Russia continues aerial assault as Ukraine spruiks drone advantage to allies
items.summary: Western officials and analysts say Ukrainian forces have disrupted Russia's planned spring offensive and managed to retake territory.

items.title: When AI starts shopping for you, fashion may be entering a new era of pricing
items.summary: Dynamic pricing is already changing the final cost of clothing. Some prices are rising, but others are falling.

items.title: Sydney Youth Orchestras' visit to Wilcannia brings together country and city kids who all love music
items.summary: The Sydney Youth Orchestras travelled to this tiny town to help inspire the local kids, but when they left, they were the ones who felt changed.

items.title: Pop culture quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and ER?
items.summary: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor ER?

items.title: Israel takes out key bridge in Lebanon, Hegseth threatens Iranian infrastructure
items.summary: An Israeli strike has severed the last bridge linking southern Lebanon to the rest of the country ahead of mooted talks between Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

items.title: Uncertainty over fuel supplies after major fire at oil refinery in Geelong
items.summary: An "unprecedented" fire has broken out at one of two remaining oil refineries producing fuel for Australians, with residents in parts of Geelong told to stay indoors due to smoke.

items.title: Defence spending to lift by $53 billion over the next decade as government warns of 'intensifying' risks
items.summary: Australia's defence spending will climb by $53 billion over the next decade amid what the federal government says are "intensifying" global risks.

items.title: Andrew Mungatopi's trial for alleged murder of Tiwi Islands sistergirl begins in NT Supreme Court
items.summary: Jurors in the murder trial of a man accused of fatally stabbing a 37-year-old sistergirl from the Tiwi Islands have heard there was "no bad blood" between them in the lead-up to the "family tragedy".

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: WA Food Minister Jackie Jarvis warns of 'challenging times ahead' for groceries
items.summary: WA Agriculture and Food Minister Jackie Jarvis says natural disasters as well as fuel and fertiliser availability and cost increases are contributing to real concerns around food supply in WA.

items.title: Darwin middle school teacher who broke female student's teeth found guilty of causing serious harm
items.summary: A former Darwin Middle School teacher who struck a female student in the face, breaking her teeth, after she demanded her confiscated phone back has been found guilty by a jury of unlawfully causing serious harm.

items.title: Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. That's not the point
items.summary: More than a day after Angus Taylor unveiled the first plank of the Coalition's long-awaited migration policy and many media interviews later, it's still unclear what the opposition is actually proposing to change.

items.title: Anthony Albanese meets with Sultan of Brunei to shore up fertiliser and fuel supplies
items.summary: Brunei may be able to help Australia weather the economic chaos caused by the Iran war by sending additional supplies of fertiliser, the prime minister says.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade could turn into global agrifood crisis
items.summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations says ships in the Strait of Hormuz need to start moving again to avoid a global agrifood crisis. Here's why.

items.title: Farmers selling cattle in record numbers as feed becomes scarce and El Niño forecast
items.summary: More than 500,000 head of cattle have already been sold across New South Wales this year, with Scone seeing its biggest sale in 20 years.

items.title: Youths pleading guilty to crimes they may not have committed under new bail laws, NSW inquiry hears
items.summary: Several lawyers have told a state parliament inquiry into the youth justice system that bail laws are encouraging children to plead guilty to crimes they may not have committed.

items.title: Trucking body calls for immediate action from federal government to stem business closures
items.summary: Trucking industry peak group NatRoad calls on the federal government to urgently implement the $1 billion Economic Resilience Program it promised two weeks ago.

items.title: Power confirms it will appeal after AFL Tribunal releases reasons for Zak Butters guilty finding
items.summary: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

items.title: PNG hotel owner looks to sue Australian government for $11m in unpaid refugee accommodation costs
items.summary: A hotel in Papua New Guinea is attempting to sue the Australian government over accommodation costs for refugees previously detained on Manus Island.

items.title: Iran war's exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback
items.summary: Projects are in the works to expand Australia's domestic fertiliser supply as prices rise by 60 per cent.

items.title: Agriculture expert says fertiliser shortage has lesser domestic impact on food supply

items.title: Kanye West postpones show in France until further notice
items.summary: US rapper Kanye West has postponed his upcoming concert in the French city of Marseille, following reports that the country's interior minister was seeking to block the performance.

items.title: Tropical cyclone Sinlaku causes significant damage in Northern Mariana Islands and Guam
items.summary: A super typhoon with ferocious winds and heavy rains made landfall in the Commonwealth of Northern Mariana Islands (CNMI) overnight, destroying homes and infrastructure.

items.title: Earthquake captured on camera in bird nesting box in Orange
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Wright Prospecting awarded royalties from Hope Downs mine after legal battle with Gina Rinehart
items.summary: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

items.title: Frenchman scoops up 1-million-euro Picasso painting in Paris raffle
items.summary: A man from Paris has won a Picasso painting worth 1 million euros ($1.6 million) with a 100-euro raffle ticket.

items.title: Victorian independent candidates win High Court battle over cap on political donations
items.summary: Victoria's political donation and disclosure laws have been scrapped by the High Court just seven months out from the state's upcoming election.

items.title: How white should your teeth actually be?
items.summary: Experts say there are many factors that influence how white a tooth appears, and it's not just about whitening or what we’re eating.

items.title: Newmont's Cadia gold mine suspends underground operations after earthquake in Orange
items.summary: All underground workers at Newmont's Cadia gold mine have been brought to the surface as inspections are carried out following an earthquake.

items.title: King Charles to woo Trump and restore 'closest of friendships' on US visit
items.summary: King Charles will have tea and a private meeting with US President Donald Trump during his US state visit later this month, with trips to Washington, New York and Virginia.

items.title: Super Rugby Pacific's Moana Pasifika say 2026 season will be team's last due to finances and other challenges
items.summary: Citing financial, operational, and strategic barriers that have made continuing unviable, battling Super Rugby Pacific outfit Moana Pasifika reveal they will disband at season's end.
